user_query:
Please set up a daily scheduled task for our annual park rangers knowledge exchange that: (a) downloads and extracts content from official public wildland fire information pages, (b) composes a concise status summary, and (c) drafts a daily briefing email. Use the provided input files under input/. Produce the artifacts under output/ and write a runnable script at scripts/daily_task.sh that can be invoked manually and via a cron entry.

Requirements:
- Inputs (mandatory):
  - input/domains.yaml: list of official domains and topic hints to target.
  - input/keywords.json: list of keywords to count in page text.
  - input/recipients.csv: recipients for the daily briefing.
  - input/meeting_context.md: context for the annual knowledge exchange.
  - input/template.md: email template with placeholders to fill.

- On each run, do the following:
  1) For each domain item in input/domains.yaml, fetch one publicly accessible landing page on that domain whose primary topic aligns with the provided topic_hint (prefer a path that contains the hint; if none is found, use the domain’s homepage). Do not hardcode specific URLs in the code or the outputs. Save the raw HTML to output/web/{domain_slug}.html, where domain_slug is the domain with dots replaced by dashes (e.g., nps-gov.html). Extract and record per source: final URL resolved after any redirects, HTTP status code, page <title>, counts of each keyword (case-insensitive) from input/keywords.json in the visible text, and total text length (characters or words). If a page cannot be fetched, still record an entry with the error noted.
  2) Write output/metrics/summary.json containing an array of objects with fields: domain, final_url, status_code, title, text_length, keyword_counts (a mapping of keyword to integer count), and errors (if any).
  3) Compose output/reports/daily_status_YYYY-MM-DD.md with fixed sections in this order: "Sources checked" (list each domain and its final_url and status_code), "Keyword counts per source" (per domain, show counts for each keyword from input/keywords.json), "Notable notes" (up to 3 sentences from any fetched page that include at least one keyword; if none are available, write "No notable sentences"), and "Next actions" (summarize from input/meeting_context.md).
  4) Draft output/email/daily_brief_YYYY-MM-DD.eml using input/template.md by filling placeholders so that: the Subject line includes the date and the total keyword hits across all sources; the To header lists names and emails from input/recipients.csv; the body contains a brief overview referencing input/meeting_context.md, links as file paths to each output/web/{domain_slug}.html file and to output/reports/daily_status_YYYY-MM-DD.md; and a short closing. Do not attempt to send the email; only write the draft file.
  5) Write output/logs/run_YYYY-MM-DDTHHMMSSZ.json with run_timestamp (UTC), files_written (list of all output paths created), sources_attempted (list of domains), and any errors encountered.

- Scheduling:
  - Generate output/schedule/cron.txt containing a single cron line that would run the task every day at 07:15 local time by invoking scripts/daily_task.sh with any needed arguments (e.g., --outdir output). Do not install the cron job; just write the line.
  - scripts/daily_task.sh must run the full workflow (steps 1–5) using the provided input files and produce all the outputs above when executed once.

- Constraints:
  - Use only official public sources as identified by domain in input/domains.yaml. Do not embed direct URLs in the request or outputs; store only the resolved URLs discovered at runtime in the metrics and report.
  - Internet access is required to fetch pages; if a fetch fails, the run must still complete with logged errors and partial outputs.
  - All outputs must be deterministic given the inputs and the pages retrieved on that run and must be written under the specified output/ paths.

Deliverables:
- scripts/daily_task.sh (executable script that performs the workflow)
- output/web/{domain_slug}.html (one per domain)
- output/metrics/summary.json
- output/reports/daily_status_YYYY-MM-DD.md
- output/email/daily_brief_YYYY-MM-DD.eml
- output/logs/run_YYYY-MM-DDTHHMMSSZ.json
- output/schedule/cron.txt

You can choose any language or shell tooling. Please ensure that running scripts/daily_task.sh once in a normal local workspace produces all of the above artifacts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
